textPlain:
I just got blocked by the CEO of ZoomInfo for documenting surveillance 
infrastructure on their GTM Studio landing page.Timeline:
1. CEO posts product demo on LinkedIn
2. I analyze the landing page with Chrome DevTools
3. I post findings in comments (40+ cookies pre-consent, biometrics, etc.)
4. CEO blocks me within minutesSo I'm releasing the full evidence pack publicly:
https://github.com/clark-prog/blackout-publicWhat I found:
- Sardine.ai behavioral biometrics (mouse/typing patterns) firing before consent
- PerimeterX device fingerprinting pre-consent
- 118 unique tracking domains on a single page load
- Base64-encoded config showing "enableBiometrics: true"
- Formal partnership with Sardine (partnerId: "zoominfo")The irony: ZoomInfo sells visitor identification tools but uses 3 external fingerprinting vendors on their own site.All evidence is reproducible. HAR files, deobfuscated code, legal analysis included.AMA about findings or methodology.
